steps.title: Set the numerator equal to zero
steps.content:
A fraction equals $0$ only when its numerator equals $0$ and its denominator is not $0$. Use the numerator:

$$(x-6)(x-11)=0$$

steps.title: Find the valid solutions
steps.content:
Set each factor equal to $0$:

$$x-6=0,\quad x=6$$
$$x-11=0,\quad x=11$$

The denominator excludes $x=5$, so both solutions are valid.

steps.title: Add the solutions
steps.content:
The question asks for the sum:

$$6+11=17$$

The answer is C.
